papers.title: DroneShield-AI: A Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion Framework for Real-Time Autonomous Drone Threat Detection, Behavioral Intent Classification, and Swarm Intelligence in Contested Airspace
papers.abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) threats have emerged as a defining security challenge of the 21st century. This paper presents DroneShield-AI, a unified open framework integrating six processing layers: RF signal classification, acoustic motor-signature detection, YOLOv8-based visual detection, evidence-weighted sensor fusion, a Behavioral Intent Classification Engine (BICE), and a Graph Neural Network Swarm Intelligence Module (GNN-SIM). BICE introduces the first systematic six-class threat taxonomy for drone flight patterns, enabling predictive operator alerts with a 30-second advance-warning horizon. GNN-SIM is the first open framework for adversarial multi-drone formation analysis using Graph Attention Networks. Evaluated on three publicly available real-world datasets, the fused pipeline achieves 96.1% detection accuracy, 3.2% false alarm rate, AUC-ROC: 0.981, and 142ms end-to-end latency on commodity CPU-class hardware at approximately $500-$780 USD total system cost. All code, model weights, and simulation datasets are publicly released at submission.

papers.title: Cross-Modal Benchmarking for Robotic Perception in Natural Environments
papers.abstract: Natural environments present a complex challenge to robotics perception systems. Current models, particularly vision foundation models, are largely trained on structured, urban environments leading to weaknesses in their perception for field robotics tasks. We showcase the limitations of current models using our recently released WildCross benchmark, a new cross-modal benchmark for place recognition and metric depth estimation in large-scale natural environments. WildCross comprises over 476K sequential RGB frames with semi-dense depth and surface normal annotations, each aligned with accurate 6DoF pose and synchronized dense lidar submaps. In this work, we provide an expanded analysis of the benchmark results from the recent WildCross benchmark, with particular emphasis on expanded metric depth estimation experiments. Access to the code repository and dataset for this work can be found at https://csiro-robotics.github.io/WildCross.

papers.title: Energy-Conserved Neural Pipelines: Attenuating Error Propagation in Modular Neural Networks via Physical Conservation Constraints
papers.abstract: Modular neural network pipelines suffer from error compounding: noise at any module boundary propagates and potentially amplifies through subsequent modules. We introduce energy conservation as a hard physical constraint on inter-module information flow. Activation energy (the squared L2 norm of feature vectors) is enforced to be exactly preserved at every module boundary. Unlike soft energy penalties, conservation is an inviolable law: the network may redistribute energy across neurons but cannot create or destroy it. Four experiments on CIFAR-10 demonstrate: (1) conservation retains 77.4% of clean accuracy at noise sigma=0.2, versus 35.1% for baselines and 30.9% for energy-penalized models (p<0.001, 5 seeds); (2) pipelines become depth-invariant, retaining 93.3% at depths 2 through 5 with noise at every boundary; (3) the advantage generalizes to systematic bias (+45.1%), Gaussian (+40.4%), and adversarial noise (+4.8%), with a principled non-effect on dropout (-0.3%); (4) on ResNet-18, the conservation advantage scales inversely with intrinsic normalization: +0.3 pp with BatchNorm, +26.2 pp without at sigma=0.2, reaching +58.0 pp at sigma=0.5. Experiment 5 validates the operator on a real modular robotic pipeline (MuJoCo physics, Franka Panda). Across three independent runs on separate machines (90 trials per cell), conservation provides +18.9 pp average advantage on monocular-depth-style noise. A formal bound proves conserved noise energy is strictly less than input noise energy.

executive_summary: Multi-robot and informative planning methods advance motion planning and navigation for ground, aerial, surface, and marine robots, emphasizing safety, belief sharing, and long-horizon trajectory optimization.

New learning-based planners and world models (ILD, Fibration-RRT, ConsistencyPlanner, VLGA) focus on scaling to high dimensions and complex driving scenarios while retaining real-time performance and safety.
Dexterous and contact-rich manipulation see progress via new VLA adaptations, world models, tactile sensing, and Real2Sim2Real pipelines that better handle morphology gaps and dynamics uncertainty.

Vision-Language(-Action) architectures are being refined with better action pretraining, multimodal timing, language steering, and test-time compute allocation to improve robustness and instruction/OOD generalization.

Real-world deployment concerns—sim-to-real gaps, safety, adversarial robustness, and human-in-the-loop efficiency—are addressed in social navigation, marine and aerial systems, surgical and industrial manipulation, and HiL-RL.

Benchmarks, sensors, and hardware frameworks (DuoBench, HiPi, novel tactile and piezoresistive arrays, AerialClaw, MARUS extensions) aim to standardize evaluation and make advanced capabilities more reproducible.

institution_matches.title: Ambient Diffusion Policy: Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Data in Robotics
institution_matches.overview: Ambient Diffusion Policy introduces a noise-dependent co-training strategy that selectively uses suboptimal demonstrations only at high and low diffusion times, leveraging the spectral structure of robot action data. This separation lets policies extract useful global and local patterns from low-quality data without inheriting harmful artifacts, improving performance across noisy, mismatched, and large-scale heterogeneous datasets such as Open X-Embodiment.

topic_matches.title: Fibration Trees: A Unified Approach to Multi-Robot Motion Planning
topic_matches.institution: H Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden; KTH Royal Institute of Technology; Rice University; TH Royal Institute
topic_matches.overview: This work formalizes "fibration trees" as a unifying abstraction over projections and decompositions in multi-robot motion planning, capturing sequential prioritization, parallel decomposition, and task-space projections in a single structure. Building on this, Fibration-RRT is a probabilistically complete sampling-based planner that efficiently solves high-DoF multi-robot problems (up to 96 DoF) by exploiting user-defined fibration hierarchies.

topic_matches.title: Learning Unions of Convex Sets via Invertible Latent Decomposition for Path Planning
topic_matches.overview: ILD jointly learns an invertible mapping and a union of convex polytopes in a latent space, enabling planners to operate over explicit convex regions while retaining scalability to complex geometries. The method achieves high planning success across 2D navigation and high-DoF (6–14 DoF) manipulation, with test-time refinement eliminating observed false-positive collisions and supporting real-time collision-free motion on physical arms.

topic_matches.title: KinematicRL: A Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning Framework For Social Navigation With Kinodynamic Feasibility
topic_matches.institution: University of Science and Technology of China
topic_matches.overview: KinematicRL formulates DRL actions as second-order controls tailored to differential-drive robots and pretrains policies with stochastic iLQR, reducing the sim-to-real gap for social navigation. Coupled with a LiDAR-only human tracking pipeline and a residual gating block for balancing reactive and memory-based behaviors, the framework yields dynamically feasible navigation policies deployable with minimal real-world adjustment.

topic_matches.title: MPPI-based Informative Trajectory Planning for Search and Capture of Drifting Targets with ASVs
topic_matches.institution: Delft University of Technology; Norwegian University of Science and Technology
topic_matches.overview: The authors propose a hybrid planning framework for autonomous surface vehicles that uses MPPI-based informative planning to jointly optimize long-horizon search and tracking of drifting targets. A multi-objective cost encodes exploration, interception, and safety, with a switch to pure-pursuit guidance for physical capture; simulations and field trials demonstrate superior target capture efficiency over baseline planners.

topic_matches.title: UGV-Conditioned Multi-UAV Informative Planning on a Shared Exposure Belief
topic_matches.overview: This work coordinates multiple UAVs to reduce a UGV's exposure to threats while navigating through uncertain hazard fields by maintaining a shared exposure belief updated from aerial observations. A spatial region-assignment strategy allocates UAV sensing to route-relevant areas, yielding large reductions in UGV cumulative exposure and redundant aerial coverage in simulation compared to hazard-agnostic reconnaissance.

topic_matches.title: VLGA: Vision-Language-Geometry-Action Models for Autonomous Driving
topic_matches.overview: VLGA augments VLA driving policies with a geometry expert supervised to reconstruct dense per-pixel pointmaps from LiDAR, explicitly grounding actions in 3D structure. On nuScenes and Bench2Drive it achieves state-of-the-art VLA performance in both open- and closed-loop evaluations, substantially reducing trajectory error and collision rates without relying on ego-state inputs.

topic_matches.title: ConsistencyPlanner: Real-time Planning with Fast-Sampling Consistency Models
topic_matches.overview: ConsistencyPlanner leverages fast-sampling consistency models to generate diverse candidate trajectories for autonomous driving and fuses heterogeneous scene and action features via an attention-based decoder. The approach balances multimodality with real-time performance, improving safety metrics in challenging dynamic scenarios compared to prior learning-based planners.

topic_matches.title: Traceable Virtual Sea Trials in the Marine Robotics Unity Simulator for Manoeuvring Assessment of Unmanned Surface Vehicles
topic_matches.overview: Extending the MARUS simulator, the authors build a standardized virtual sea-trial framework that automates IMO/ITTC-compliant Turning Circle and Zig-Zag manoeuvres for USVs. The system emphasizes traceable command-actuation logging and SI-ready data conditioning, enabling repeatable hydrodynamic-derivative identification and digital-twin calibration without costly physical sea trials.
